children.label: Many people are unhappy with Linkedin
LinkedIn inbox is a poor networking tool. Messages go unread, no prioritization, creates noise not signal
children.test: Mom tests across AEs, founders, VCs — inbox usage & frustration
children.reason: Most people didn't feel it's an issue. The ones that have a lot of linkedin reachout use external tools for it anyway.

children.label: Mutual connections is inaccurate — they see 300+ but have no intelligence on which paths are real
children.test: Behavioral: how often do people see / click mutual connections? What for?

children.label: Option B: Strategic Reach — find the real intro path to a specific high-value target
children.test: Mom tests — how often do you target a specific person for an intro?
children.reason: Frequency too low — ~2× per Q. Not a recurring daily pain.

children.label: Waterfall sequencing: try connector #1 → auto-fallback #2, prevent double-ask
children.test: Pending: validate parent node frequency first

children.label: Filter 300+ mutuals to 3–5 real paths via active relationship signals
children.test: Pending: validate parent node frequency first

children.label: Rank connectors by responsiveness to both you and the target
children.test: Pending: validate parent node frequency first

children.label: Job searchers are asking for many intros
children.test: Interview: when and why do you look at mutual connections?

children.label: Many mutual connections - helps the user build confidence before the reachout. More chances for him to accept.
children.test: Interview: when and why do you look at mutual connections?

children.label: People check mutual connections as part of understanding the target. What people he associate with.
children.test: Interview: what triggers you to check mutual connections?

children.reason: **The overwhelming majority expressed dissatisfaction with LinkedIn.** Out of approximately 55 unique interviewees, roughly **40+ expressed negative sentiment**, while only about **8-10 spoke favorably**, with the remainder mixed.
children.test: Question all transcripts: Are people unhappy with Linkedin - I need count of people mentioning that vs. people taking favorbly about linkedin. Include names. Skip Yaniv, Matan and Shaul

children.label: HubSpot / newsletter approach to nurturing is broken
Founders in small companies that are wearing the sales hat are losing deals because of poor nurturing
children.test: Behavioual: founder interviews

children.label: VC principals miss deal flow — can't maintain active signals on 80–150 priority founders
children.test: 3 VC principal Mom test interviews

children.label: AEs will pay out of pocket for personal network CRM — owned by the rep, not the company
children.test: AE validation interviews (simulation + real calls) + cold email signal

children.label: CSMs need relationship intelligence to prevent churn and surface expansion opportunities
children.test: Cold email signal — real call interviews pending
test

children.label: Introductions & intro-meetings are a viral product space
Option A: Intro Marketplace — product requires both sides; asker and giver both need network intelligence to complete the workflow
children.test: Build two-sided intro flow — does the asker naturally recruit the giver?

children.label: Asker sees which of helper's connections are real & active before asking
children.test: Simulation: does seeing the real map change ask behavior?

children.label: Giver shares a real map of available connections — turns vague asks into actionable requests
children.test: Simulation: does shared map increase intro success rate?

children.label: Option C: Meeting Intro Engine — pre-meeting briefs create two-sided value; counterpart email is the viral moment of conversion
children.test: Behavioral: do people practice the idea of asking for 2-3 introductions in every meeting

children.label: Gordy Strategic Ask Brief creates enough value that users share it internally with colleagues
children.test: Internal: we need to bring users from companies with actual employees

children.label: Counterpart email creates desire in someone who's never heard of InTouch — before they have a product to land on
children.test: Live emails to real attendees — measuring clicks, replies, sign-up intent

children.label: Auto compose intro request feature is used and users don't delete the attribution
children.test: Data from live experiments + users interview

children.label: Post-meeting Gordy follow-up converts successful meetings into new warm user acquisition
children.test: Pending: define loop after counterpart email signal confirmed

children.label: Name drop test: "Gordy mentioned you know X — may I mention you when I reach out?" creates warm outreach at scale
children.test: Live Gordy outreach to founders, VCs, AEs — tracking Yes/No + follow-up calls

children.label: Name drop works with founders and VCs — strong positive signal confirmed
children.test: Name drop test run on founder + investor network

children.label: Name drop works with AEs and sales professionals — test running
children.test: Name drop test on AE network — running
Past successes
children.reason: Calendly, zoom, Doodle (10M monthly users), Superhuman, Fathom, Firefly, Boardy, ...

children.label: Knowing something personal about a target meaningfully improves cold outreach conversion rates
children.test: A/B test: compare cold outreach with pokemon-sourced personal intel vs generic outreach — does conversion rate differ significantly?

children.label: Users doing cold outreach have no scalable way to get public personal intel on targets today — they do it manually or skip it entirely
children.test: Mom test: do people research targets before cold outreach? How long does it take? Have they tried to automate it? Would they pay to get it instantly?

children.label: Personal contact intelligence improves nurturing by creating more follow-up opportunities, increasing engagement rates, and ultimately improving win rates
children.test: Behavioral interview: do people who know personal details about contacts report better relationship outcomes? Simulation: compare nurture sequences with vs without personal intel.

children.label: Personal intel on a connector makes intro requests more targeted and more likely to succeed
children.test: Simulation: compare intro request quality and success rate with vs without personal context on the connector

children.label: Users will pay to mine deep personal intel from their own emails, call transcripts, and WhatsApp to fuel warm relationship actions
children.test: Waitlist/credits test: when offered pokemon mining, do users connect their email or transcripts? Measure activation rate and willingness to pay.
